key_points: Social reform movements in the 19th century aimed to remove harmful social practices
Many reformers worked to improve the status of women in society
Child marriage and restrictions on widows were major social problems
Education for women was encouraged to improve their independence
Reform movements also tried to reduce caste discrimination
examples: Reformers encouraged girls to attend schools
Lower caste communities demanded equal rights
Newspapers and books spread reform ideas
Public debates helped people question harmful customs
important_concepts: Social reform – efforts to improve society by removing harmful practices
Women's education – education provided to girls and women to empower them
Caste discrimination – unfair treatment of people based on caste
Equality – fair treatment of all individuals regardless of background
Social justice – ensuring fairness and equal rights in society
exam_tips: Remember the main social problems faced by women in the 19th century
Understand the importance of women's education in reform movements
Learn the meaning of caste discrimination and equality
Focus on how print culture helped spread reform ideas
Write answers with clear examples from the chapter
